summary: First end-to-end test of OraOne V1. Backend: 18/18 pytest tests passing across root, auth (register/login/me/logout/forgot/refresh), agents CRUD (voice/chat/whatsapp), leads CRUD, dashboard overview (authed + 401 unauthed), onboarding flag flip, contact and newsletter persistence. Frontend: 29/29 critical UI checks passing — home hero, all 11 marketing/legal routes, 404 page, admin login redirect to /app/overview, all 9 sidebar nav tabs route correctly, agent create flow (select type → Next → AgentBuilder with 5 tabs + Save), leads export button, KB upload UI, integrations Connect/Disconnect buttons render, forgot-password input present, logout redirect to /login, protected route guard redirects unauthenticated users to /login, signup with random email lands on /onboarding/agent.

minor.issue: When CORS_ORIGINS='*', allow_credentials is forced to False. This is harmless for the current preview deployment (frontend and backend share the same host so cookies are sent same-origin), but if CORS_ORIGINS is ever set to an explicit cross-origin list it must be a specific origin list, not '*' — code already handles that branch correctly. Worth documenting.

issues: Recharts logs repeated warnings: 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' on initial mount. Charts still render correctly in the screenshot, but the warning suggests the ResponsiveContainer parent briefly has 0 size during first paint. Consider giving the chart container explicit min-height or aspect ratio.

action_items: (Optional) Add min-height to ResponsiveContainer wrappers to remove Recharts width/height warnings on first paint.
(Optional) Document CORS behavior in README — wildcard origins disable cookie credentials; preview works because of same-origin.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~520 lines — still readable but approaching split point; consider routers/auth.py, routers/agents.py.
All MongoDB queries correctly exclude _id via projection ({'_id': 0}) — good.
Passwords hashed with bcrypt ($2b$...) via bcrypt.gensalt() — correct format.
httpOnly cookies set with secure=True, samesite='none' — appropriate for HTTPS preview.
Seed-admin path correctly upserts password if changed (verify_password fallback) — idempotent.
No brute-force lockout / rate limiting on /auth/login — acceptable for V1 mock but worth flagging.
Forgot-password leaks token only to backend log (no SES) — by design per spec.
Pydantic EmailStr will 422 on unusual TLDs (e.g., '.test'); not a bug but tests must use real-looking emails.
seed_data_creation: Tests create and clean up agents/leads/contact/newsletter using TEST_ prefix; signup created e2e_<rand>@example.com users (left in DB, low volume).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest file at /app/backend/tests/test_oraone_backend.py covers all CRUD + auth flows. Admin seed works correctly even if password rotated. Frontend testids are centralized in /app/frontend/src/constants/testIds.js; note that /app/agents/new uses 'agent-create-type-{voice|chat|whatsapp}' and 'agent-create-next' (NOT the onboarding-step1-* ids which belong to /onboarding/agent). AgentBuilder tabs use 'builder-tab-{basic|config|knowledge|integrations|review}'. Mocked: social login, conversation transcripts, KB file upload, email send, integrations Connect (state-only).
